segments.text: So, the post boxes are installed in different localities to facilitate that people can easily post letters.
They don't have to come all the way, travel long distances to and come and put the letter in the post box.
it is difficult for us to approach Him.
when a man dies, we cannot see how the spiritual form leaves the body.

We are very gross in our vision and we don't see spirit, soul.
But we know soul is a reality.
And the Supreme Soul, Supreme Lord, He is all spiritual.
He has transcendental body, all spiritual body.
And with our blunt material senses, with our eyes, we cannot perceive the Lord.
We know that the Lord is present everywhere.
But still, there are many people who believe that God does not exist.
Even people like Rinnekar Shripur, very powerful people, they challenged openly, where is God?
The Lord has created this world that if someone wants to live life with the understanding that God does not exist,
there are enough reasons for him to be convinced of that fact.
That's a different thing that Maya has to take away his intelligence.
Otherwise, little bit we are introspective, we can easily make out that,
yes, behind this wonderful creation there has to be an intelligent creator.
Nothing wonderful comes about from nowhere.
So as devotees, we know the Lord is there.
But unfortunately, we have material vision.
We don't have spiritual senses awakened.
And hence, we cannot perceive the Lord in our present, with our present material senses.
So just like the crude example of a postal department installing post boxes,
there is the Lord, mercifully descends in archa-vigraha forms,
which are made of material elements, apparently, looks like made of stone, wood, metal.
But because of omnipotency of the Lord, they are all spiritual.
They are non-different from the Lord.
Prabhupada gives mentions in one place.
The deity, although we know that it is made from stone, he is not stone.
By offering food to the deity and by decorating and serving the deity,
one gets the same result that one derives from serving the Lord personally in Vaikuntha.
Just like posting the letter in the general post office,
and posting the letter in the local post box, there is no difference.
There may be some minor delay.
So it is the magnanimity of the Lord that the Lord descends in the form of archa-vigraha.
My devotees always see the smiling face of my form,
like the rising, morning rising sun.
Now, mayavadis or impersonalists who do not believe in the personal form of the Lord,
they don't believe in these statements.
For many of them, deity worship is nothing but idol worship,
But devotees knowing that Lord is so magnanimous, so great, so kind, so loving,
so merciful, that He agrees to come.
If He wants to come, we cannot stop.
When a pure devotee invites Him, He agrees to come in a form made of material elements.
One derives the same benefit of serving the Lord personally in Vaikuntha.
If we make a garland and offer it to the Lord,
it is the same as someone offering garland to Krishna in the spiritual world in Vaikuntha, Goloka Vrindavan.
If someone looks at the lotus feet of the Lord, of the deity,
and meditates on it, it is as good as meditating on the lotus feet of the Supreme Lord in Vaikuntha.
it is as good as being in the personal presence of the Lord.
Just like Arjuna was very fortunate in the battle of Kurukshetra,
through various transcendental forms like the deity, like the holy name,

the deities sometimes express their mind.

So when we chant, when we hear proper shravanam, kirtanam, all these things, then we develop
the right qualification. Even the holy name of the Lord, many people say, what's the use?
It's an ordinary sound. How can it be the Lord? One time there was a small little girl,
she was disturbing her mother when the mother was cooking. And while the cooking was on,
she would be all, you know, creating ruckus sometimes, crying, disturbing the mother.
So mother called the girl and said, do one thing, go around the house, if you see any
please help me clean up, it's time for father to return. Please help me clean up the house.
So the girl also felt involved, the mother had given something responsible to do. So
she started moving around the house and picking up the pieces of paper which were lying on
the floor and throwing in the dustbin. So all along, mother was keeping an eye on the
girl. And she was happy that, you know, she was doing something meaningful. And as soon
as the mother was actively preparing the list,
As soon as she saw one time, she was about to pick up a piece of paper, tear it, and mother says, stop, stop, stop.
So the girl became a little surprised, what is this? So many pieces of paper I have thrown.
She was very happy, she was encouraging me, and now this piece of paper, she's saying, stop, stop, stop.
So the girl couldn't understand, so mother said, this is not an ordinary piece of paper.
This is a 500 rupee note. Maybe the father in the morning, when he would have gone to the office, he would have slipped out of his wallet.
He says, wait, wait, wait. So the girl could not understand. After all, it's a piece of paper.
So then mother had to explain to the girl how it's not an ordinary piece of paper.
So she said, because sometimes you have to speak to a person in their own language.
Beta, this piece of paper, how to explain what is 500 rupee note?
With this piece of paper, on your birthday, you can get 500 chocolates.
Oh, is it? The girl is inexperienced. For her, all pieces of paper, including currency notes, are the same.
Likewise, for an inexperienced person, the name of the Lord is an ordinary sound vibration.
Like any other word we utter, it's basically some vocal cords interacting and some sound being produced.
Just like in a mundane example, we have pieces of paper which are worthless.
You can put in the dustbin, you can trash it, and you also have pieces of paper which are worth.
With that 2000 rupee note, you can buy reams and reams of paper.
Likewise, by the Lord's omnipotency, just like here it is said,
my benevolent forms, the Lord has descended in the form of his holy name.
For the faithless, for the person whose heart is stone-hearted, it's an ordinary sound.
The deity is made of stone, is made of wood.
For a devotee whose consciousness is awakened because of shravanam, kirtanam, by chanting, by reading and all that,
that sound vibration is none other than Krishna himself.
So MPB also many times in his seminars, in his workshop on japa, he mentions that when we chant, we can chant mechanically.
We can also chant with the consciousness that the Lord has descended on my tongue.
Just like when a person who is very, very prominent, a VIP comes, assuming that we have a president's visit or prime minister visit to the temple,
we'll all become so attentive to receive him.
So many arrangements will be made.
Depending on the stature of the person, we will make arrangements.
Now if we don't have proper shravanam, when the Lord descends in the form of holy name,
we will be very casual, very reckless, very inattentive.
When we have that consciousness, that understanding that the Lord has descended,
it is Lord's magnanimity that the Lord has come on my tongue.
And it's an opportunity for us.
His intention is so that we can associate with him, we can touch with him, touch him and come out of this material existence.
Same crude example, the post office installing post boxes.
The Lord agreeing to come just by uttering his name.
And we as devotees have to take advantage of such opportunity which the Lord has given us.
Yesterday we were hearing, this is a very interesting thing which Madhavapura said that
when we chant the holy name, Krishna says surrender unto me.
Maa mekam sharanam maa sucha, do not fear, I'll take care.
So many times, you know, it's a very vague thing, what is surrender?
Although we know shastras give principles of surrender, so many things.
But very practical thing which was discussed yesterday,
the Lord is being merciful, he is coming out of his magnanimity.
He is descending in the form of his name.
He is not expecting too much.
He is expecting us to surrender.
Which Krishna also says in the Gita.
And especially during the chanting time,
he is expecting surrender of our,
Many times we stop chanting, we start discussing with someone else something.
Something may be relation with service, sometime it may not be related to service.
Just because we want to discuss something.
It could be related to service also.
In the Gita he says, surrender, I will take care, maa sucha, do not fear.
Even when we have to think about our service.
Even if we have some pressure, some anxiety.
Many times during chanting time we are thinking of service.
During service time we have one hand in the japa mala.
So in that sense, even so called karmis they say, do one thing at a time.
He says, human mind is wired to work very efficiently when it does one thing at a time.
Although these days we do multitasking.
We are hearing the phone, we are talking to someone, we are trying to sign some checks or whatever it is.
So chanting time, let's give that exclusive time to the Lord.
And when Krishna says that we are sincerely trying.
Even if we may not be able to do.
At least are we making a sincere attempt?
Tapo divyam shudhye brahma saukhyam tona.
Krishna says, with that tapasya will get purified.
Are we really desiring very hard?
The example we gave, that person, just he was so eager to hear that sound.
People in this material world for some material mundane goals work so hard.
They are so passionately, you know, pursuing something which they eagerly want.
So can also, we also be determined.
I have to improve my quality of chanting.
I have to hear Bhagavatam class very attentively.
Krishna knows that we are conditioned soul.
Our mind is all infected with viruses of various sorts, various kinds of this thing.
But when he sees that we are sincere.
When he sees that we are eager.
When he sees that we are trying differently.
God helps those who help themselves.
Then he, he also helps us.
Dadami buddhi yogam tam yena mam upayanti te.
He will reveal what we should do to purify further.
So here it is said that the Lord is very magnanimous.
He has descended in the form and devotees of the Lord, they take advantage.
Which are all benevolent and they talk favorably with me.
Grantharaj Srimad Bhagavatam ki Isla Prabhupada ki.
